message:
style(window): Display impl for Appearance, type alias for handler, clippy appeasement

Six files under the vendored `frankenterm/window` crate pick up
clippy-driven cleanups. No behavior change.

## lib.rs

- `impl ToString for Appearance` → `impl std::fmt::Display for Appearance`.
  clippy's `implement_display_for_to_string` lint: manually
  implementing `ToString` is anti-pattern now that any
  `T: Display` auto-derives `ToString::to_string` via the blanket
  impl. The Display impl uses `f.write_str(name)` for each variant
  (`"Light"`, `"Dark"`, `"LightHighContrast"`, `"DarkHighContrast"`);
  callers that previously called `appearance.to_string()` continue
  to work via the blanket impl, so this is source-compatible.
- New `type WindowEventHandler = dyn FnMut(WindowEvent, &Window);`
  type alias. `WindowEventSender` previously stored
  `handler: Box<dyn FnMut(WindowEvent, &Window)>`, which clippy flags
  (`type_complexity`) and makes harder to read. The field type
  becomes `Box<WindowEventHandler>`. No public API change — the
  type alias is private to the module.

## window/src/bitmaps/mod.rs

Three `assert!` macros in tests that used `{err:#}` format-arg
capture are widened to also pass `err = err` explicitly, so the
message still renders when the surrounding macro expands under a
tighter rustc lint. These are test-only assertion message tweaks
with no semantic change.

## window/src/egl.rs

The module-level `#[allow(non_camel_case_types, clippy::
unreadable_literal)]` attribute grows a third lint:
`clippy::missing_transmute_annotations`. `gl_generator` emits
`mem::transmute(...)` calls without annotating the destination
function-pointer type, and the allow was needed to keep the crate
compiling on a newer toolchain — harmless because the generator's
output is machine-reviewed, not hand-edited.

## window/examples/async.rs

`glium::Frame::new(Rc::clone(&gl), ...)` becomes
`glium::Frame::new(Rc::clone(gl), ...)` — `gl` in the surrounding
scope is already an `&Rc<...>` binding, so the extra `&` forced a
redundant borrow that clippy's `redundant_borrow` flagged.

## os/macos/connection.rs

Reordered `#[cfg(test)] mod software_version_tests { ... }` so it
sits after the non-test `impl ConnectionOps`, `pub fn
nsscreen_to_screen_info`, and `extern "C" { fn NSBeep(); }` items
— clippy's `items_after_test_module` (the lint that reminds you
that `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` should normally live at the end of
the file because any items after it can be mistaken for test
items). File content is otherwise byte-identical — the tests
assert the same `product_build_version`, `product_user_visible_version`,
and `product_name` round-trip through `plist::from_bytes` and the
same `"macOS 14.0 (23A344)"` connection-name format.

## os/macos/window.rs

- `if state.is_ok() { conn.gl_connection.borrow_mut()
  .replace(Rc::clone(state.as_ref().unwrap().get_connection())); }`
  becomes `if let Ok(state) = &state { conn.gl_connection
  .borrow_mut().replace(Rc::clone(state.get_connection())); }`
  (clippy `unnecessary_unwrap`; no change in behaviour, just
  idiomatic Ok-unwrap-via-pattern).
- `set_window_position` drops three `as f64` casts where the RHS
  was already `f64` (`cartesian.x as f64 - delta_x` →
  `cartesian.x - delta_x`, etc.). clippy `unnecessary_cast`.
- `drop_inner` changes `this.set_ivar(VIEW_CLS_NAME,
  std::ptr::null_mut() as *mut c_void)` to
  `this.set_ivar(VIEW_CLS_NAME, std::ptr::null_mut::<c_void>())` —
  the turbofish moves the type into the function call instead of
  using an `as` cast, which clippy `cast_ptr_alignment`/`ptr_cast`
  prefers.

No observable runtime difference; nothing in the public API
changes.
